creator: alt2600@icloud.com
text:
(In reply to Fernando Apesteguía from comment #1)

I'm confused and not seeing any conflicts as described. The attached patch doesn't make gnuplot enabled by default, it uses gnuplot-lite by default, unless the GNUPLOT option is turned on whereby it will allow use of gnuplot instead. So I am confused how this conflicts with folks using gnuplot-lite, the default behavior remains to use lite. The folks using lite will see no changes at all unless they have an unintended dirty OPTIONS_SET in their environment.

https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/ports/ section 4.5.1 indicates I should be able to use ports. I fail to see how working with html, sgml, and pdf requires a "clean environment". There is no compilation going on here, just parsing and production of documentation using installed ports, based on the markup encoding using in the source of the documentation. Its not as if there is cmake bringing everything it finds into the mix, or even a configure running with misc/freebsd-doc-en nor the other languages. 

There was a time when I started using freebsd for my own sanity instead of gentoo where things were source built as the norm in a cleaner system than gentoo, now we seem to be shifting towards just another binary only distribution, which is unfortunate. Am I to understand that the freebsd-docs cannot or is not supported to be installed from ports on a system with anything but the most basic textproc/docproj ports installed?
